summary: The built-in user-testing validator worker exited unexpectedly twice before producing a handoff, so provider-foundation user-surface validation was completed manually via the general worker droid using the mission user-testing contract and validation API flow.

VAL-OPS-001.evidence: Timed TCP/HTTP probes on port 3102 showed repeated connection refusal before listener bind, then successful readiness at ~T+32s.

VAL-OPS-002.evidence: First successful post-bind responses for /ping, /simple/price, and /coins/markets were contract-valid rather than partial startup payloads.

VAL-OPS-007.evidence: Shutdown withdrew the listener promptly, the same port was reusable, and restart returned healthy responses after readiness.

VAL-CROSS-004.evidence: A full shutdown/restart lifecycle on port 3102 regained readiness in the expected order and did not visibly leak prior runtime state.

notes: Fallback validation was required because user-testing-validator-provider-foundation crashed twice with exit code 0 before writing a handoff.
Detailed evidence was gathered through live probes and startup/shutdown sequencing against the declared validation surface.
